skylight_Vessel.Name: FU YUAN YU 7007
Sister.Ship.Non.Compliance.Description: FU YUAN YU 019 IATTC-authorized fishing vessel— retrieving longline gear in the IATTC Convention Area about 400 miles southwest of the Mexican coast. Sea Shepherd witnessed the vessel using metal branch lines running directly from longline floats in violation of a CMM (C-16-05) that prohibits the use of this type of gear, known as shark lines.

skylight_Vessel.Name: NA
Sister.Ship.Non.Compliance.Description: LU RONG YUAN YU 668 IUU fishing in the Argentine EEZ and evaded arrest. LU RONG YUAN YU 668/977/978 disembarked dead crew members. LU RONG YUAN YU 978 transshiped without authorization in Uruguay EEZ. Forced labor indicators on the LU RONG YUAN YU 559. 6 fishing vessels in the Rongcheng Rongyuan fleet were accused of switching fishing gear types to an unauthorised type whilst operating within IOTC convention area in 2019. The vessels were recalled to China for inspection by China MOA.
